title: The original GDEW042T2 display is EOL — the GDEY042T81 replacement works, but needs PR #11 firmware
description: The original GDEW042T2 display (IL0398 controller) is end-of-life at GoodDisplay (confirmed August 2023). The replacement GDEY042T81 (SSD1683 controller) is a physical drop-in — same FPC connector, confirmed compatible by community testers. The panels require different firmware drivers, but this is handled by the assembly instructions: the firmware flash step uses PR #11, which auto-detects the display type.
resolution: Handled by the Assembly tab. The firmware flash step builds from the PR #11 branch (joeycastillo/libros#11), which auto-detects the display via busy-pin polarity. No extra steps needed — just follow the assembly guide. Note: the pre-built book.uf2 on the project site does NOT support the GDEY042T81; PR #11 is the recommended firmware source for all new builds.

title: C2 e-paper driver assembly cost is missing from every upstream BOM
description: No upstream BOM includes the fabrication and assembly cost of the C2 e-paper driver module. This is a separate pre-assembled board order — the cost depends on your fab house and volume but typically runs $30–80 per unit at small quantities. The upstream per-unit totals ($43–48) cover only the main board components.
resolution: Budget an additional $30–80 per unit for the C2 driver assembly. Get a PCBA quote from JLCPCB (using OSO-BOOK-C2-03 files) or PCBWay (using OSO-BOOK-C2-02 files) before ordering.

title: The upstream README references older file versions — this app's ordering guide is current
description: The upstream README tells builders to upload OSO-BOOK-C1-04-rounded.zip and use OSO-BOOK-C2-01 for JLCPCB. Both are outdated: the current main board gerber is C1-05 (confirmed by the schematic revision header and kitspace.yaml), and C2-01 has been replaced by C2-03 in the repo. The README also showed 1.6 mm thickness and leaded HASL in its PCBWay screenshot — both incorrect for this build.
resolution: Follow this app's ordering guide (About tab) rather than the upstream README's step-by-step. The correct files and PCB specs are listed there.

title: The upstream vision doc describes the ESP32-S3 design, not the Pico build
description: The 'why-the-open-book' document in the upstream repo opens with 'designed around the ESP32-S3' and describes a LiPo battery. The current DIY build uses the Raspberry Pi Pico with dual AAA. The ESP32-S3 design has since become the Open Book Touch — a manufactured product heading to Crowd Supply. The vision doc is the project's origin story, not a spec.
resolution: Treat the vision doc as historical context. The Pico-based build (what this app covers) and the ESP32-S3-based Open Book Touch are separate product lines.

title: BOM specifies TL1107AF180WQ (1.8 N) but the README links to 130WQ (1.3 N)
description: The upstream BOM and Digi-Key part number both specify TL1107AF180WQ — the 1.8 N actuation force variant. The README's Digi-Key link goes to TL1107AF130WQ (1.3 N, lighter press). Either variant fits the same footprint.
resolution: Either variant works. The 180WQ (1.8 N) gives a firmer click; the 130WQ (1.3 N) gives a lighter touch. Order whichever you prefer.
